verse: You created my inward parts; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Wonderful are your works. (Ps 139:13-14)

My substance was not hidden from you, when I was made in secret, and intricately formed in the lowest parts of the earth. Your eyes saw my unformed substance. All the days that were allotted for me were written in your book, when as yet none of them existed. (Ps 139:15-16)
Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you to be a prophet to the nations. (Jer 1:5)
Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, who formed you in the womb: I am the Lord, who made all things, who stretches out the heavens alone, who spreads out the earth by myself. (Is 44:24)
Thus says the Lord, who made you and formed you from the womb, who will help you: Do not be afraid, Jacob, my servant and my most righteous, whom I have chosen. (Is 44:2)

I myself also am a mortal man, like all others, and a descendant of the first one formed of earth; and in my mother’s womb I was molded into flesh, over the course of ten months, compacted in blood, from the seed of man and the pleasure of marriage. (Wis 7:1-2)
You have clothed me with skin and flesh and knit me together with bones and sinews. You have granted me life and mercy, and your visitation has preserved my spirit. (Job 10:11-12)
Give ear, you coastlands, pay attention, you peoples from afar! The Lord has called me from the womb, he named me while I was in my mother’s womb. (Is 49:1)
Just as you do not know how the spirit comes to the bones in the womb of a pregnant woman, so you do not know the works of God, who makes all things. (Eccl 11:5)
Upon you I have relied from birth: It was you who took me out of my mother’s womb: my praise shall be continually of you. (Ps 71:6)
In his hand is the soul of every living thing and the breath of all humankind. (Job 12:10)
Esau lifted up his eyes and saw the women and children, and said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “They are the children whom God has graciously given your servant.” (Gen 33:5)
Adam knew his wife Eve, and she conceived and bore Cain, saying, “I have gotten a man with the Lord’s help.” (Gen 4:1)
Isaac prayed to the Lord on behalf of his wife, because she was barren; and the Lord answered his prayer, and Rebekah his wife conceived. (Gen 25:21)
For this child I prayed, and the Lord has granted me my petition which I asked of him. Therefore I also have lent him to the Lord; as long as he lives, he shall be lent to the Lord. (1 Sam 1:27-28)

When Elizabeth heard the greeting of Mary, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit. For as soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy. (Luk 1:41,44)
Boaz took Ruth and she became his wife. When they came together, the Lord enabled her to conceive, and she bore a son. (Ruth 4:13)

The angel said to him, “Fear not, Zechariah, for your prayer is heard, and Your wife Elizabeth shall bear you a son, and you shall name him John. You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice over his birth” (Luk 1:13-14)

Then God remembered Rachel, and God heeded her prayer and opened her womb. She conceived and bore a son, and said, “God has taken away my reproach”; and she called his name Joseph, and said, “The Lord shall add to me another son!” (Gen 30:22)
The Lord visited Hannah, and she conceived and bore three sons and two daughters. (1 Sam 2:21)
The Angel said to her: “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God. Behold, you shall conceive in your womb, and you shall bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus.” (Luk 1:30-31)

After those days, Elizabeth his wife conceived, and hid herself for five months, saying, “This is how the Lord has dealt with me, when he looked favorably on me and took away my disgrace among my people.” (Luk 1:24-25)
The Lord visited Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did for Sarah as he had promised. She conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the time of which God had foretold him. (Gen 21:1-2)

They rose up in the morning early and worshiped before the Lord; then they returned and came to their house at Ramah. Elkanah knew his wife Hannah, and the Lord remembered her. In due time Hannah conceived and bore a son and called his name Samuel, saying, “I have asked for him from the Lord.” (1 Sam 1:19-20)
As arrows in the hand of a warrior, so are the children of one’s youth. Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them. (Ps 127:4)
I was cast on you from the womb, and you have been my God since my mother bore me. (Ps 22:10)

A woman when she is in labor, has pain, because her hour has come. But when she has brought forth the child, she remembers no more the anguish, because of her joy that a human being is born into the world. (Jn 16:21)
He gives the barren woman a home, and to be a joyful mother of children. (Ps 113:9)
Behold, I and the children whom the Lord has given me are signs and wonders in Israel from the Lord of hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion. (Is 8:18)

The Lord will make you abound with all goods, in the fruit of your womb, in the fruit of your cattle, and in the fruit of your land, which the Lord swore to your ancestors that he would give you. (Deut 28:11)
I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make nations of you, and kings shall come out of you. (Gen 17:6)
Children are a heritage from the Lord, and the fruit of the womb is his reward. (Ps 127:3)
She will be saved through childbearing, if she continues in faith and love, and in sanctification accompanied by self-control. (1 Tim 2:15)
Who is he who fears the Lord? He will show him the way he should choose. He will dwell in prosperity, and his offspring will inherit the land. (Ps 25:12-13)

Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem. Praise your God, O Zion. For he has strengthens the bars of your gates. He has blessed your children within you. He makes peace at your borders, and he has satisfied you with the finest of the wheat. (Ps 147:12-14)
Children’s children are the crown of old age. And the glory of children is their parents. (Pro 17:6)

God will never abandon his mercy, nor will he destroy or abolish his own works. And he will not wipe out the descendants of his elect. And he will not destroy the offspring of the one who loves him. (Sir 47:22)

Blessed is the man who fears the Lord, who delights greatly in his commandments. His offspring will be mighty on the earth. The generation of the upright will be blessed. Wealth and riches will be in his house, and his righteousness shall endure forever. (Ps 112:1-3)
The Lord bless you out of Zion. May you see the goodness of Jerusalem all the days of your life. May you see your children’s children. Peace be on Israel! (Ps 128:5-6)
Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your ground, and the fruit of your herds, the increase of your cattle and the flocks of your sheep. (Deut 28:4)
Take wives and have sons and daughters; take wives for your sons, and give your daughters to husbands, that they may bear sons and daughters; that they may increase there and not decrease. (Jer 29:6)

Thus says the Lord: Execute justice and righteousness, and deliver from the hand of the oppressor anyone who has been despoiled. And do no wrong or violence to the stranger, the orphan, and the widow, nor shed innocent blood in this place. (Jer 22:3)
Render justice to the weak and the fatherless; uphold the rights of the afflicted and the destitute. Deliver the weak and the needy; rescue them from the hand of the wicked. (Ps 82:3-4)

Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; put away the evil deeds from before my sight; cease to do evil, learn to do well; seek justice, relieve the oppressed, defend the fatherless, plead for the widow. (Is 1:16-17)
They shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; and the land was polluted with blood. (Ps 106:38)

If you fail to rescue those who are being taken away to death, those who ready to be slain; if you say, “Look, we did not know about this”— Surely, he who ponders the heart knows it and will repay all according to their deeds? (Pro 24:11-12)
Whoever sheds human blood, by a human his blood will be shed: for humankind was made in the image of God. (Gen 9:6)

Do not pollute the land of your habitation, which is stained with the blood of the innocent; neither is it able to be expiated in any way other than by the blood of him who has shed the blood of another. Do not therefore defile the land where you live and in the midst of which I dwell. For I the Lord, dwell among the Israelites. (Num 35:33-34)
You shall not kill. (Exo 20:13)
Speak out for the people who cannot speak, for the rights of all who are destitute. Speak out, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy. (Pro 31:8-9)
